frameworks.name: drift-diffusion model
frameworks.one_line: normative evidence accumulation account mapped onto distributed cortical MEG dynamics

frameworks.name: cortical hierarchy of perceptual decision-making
frameworks.one_line: decision signals distributed across sensory, parietal, frontal and motor cortex

frameworks.name: post-decisional confidence computation
frameworks.one_line: choice-locked feedback signals propagate to visual cortex to support confidence

core_question: How are perceptual decision signals dynamically distributed across the human cortical hierarchy during choice and confidence formation?
key_assumptions: perceptual choice can be approximated by drift-diffusion evidence accumulation
MEG source-localized activity indexes region- and frequency-specific decision signals
feedforward and feedback signals can be temporally dissociated around the choice
manipulations: perceptual decisions with subsequent confidence report
alignment of neural activity to stimulus vs. choice
comparison across cortical regions and frequency bands
key_findings: choice-predictive signals emerge progressively up the cortical hierarchy into motor cortex
choice-locked feedback signals propagate from association cortex back to visual cortex
distinct cortical networks carry evidence accumulation versus confidence-related signals
decision dynamics span many regions and frequency bands rather than a single locus
interpretations: perceptual decisions are implemented as large-scale distributed dynamics across cortex
post-decisional feedback to sensory cortex supports confidence computation
